daily_brief: The US-Iran war dominates global headlines as President Trump's 48-hour ultimatum on reopening the Strait of Hormuz expires Monday night, with Tehran threatening retaliatory strikes on regional infrastructure including energy facilities and desalination plants [BBC, RFI, France 24]. The IEA warned the crisis represents the worst energy shock in decades, combining 'two oil crises and one gas crash' as oil prices surge past $100 per barrel [BBC, Le Monde]. Asian markets plunged sharply, with Japan's Nikkei falling 3.6% and South Korea's Kospi nearly 6% [BBC, Nikkei Asia]. Israel announced expanded ground and air operations against Hezbollah in Lebanon, with over 1,000 killed and more than a million displaced since fighting intensified [BBC]. In European politics, France's Socialist Party retained control of Paris, Marseille, Lyon, and Lille in municipal elections, offering a boost to mainstream parties ahead of presidential elections, while Germany's CDU won Rhineland-Palatinate as the AfD achieved record western gains [BBC, RFI, Le Monde]. The UN's World Meteorological Organization confirmed 2025 recorded the highest planetary energy imbalance ever measured, with the 11 warmest years all occurring since 2015 [Le Monde, France 24].

top_stories.headline: Trump's Hormuz Ultimatum Expires as Iran Threatens Regional Infrastructure Strikes
bullets: President Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum on Saturday threatening to 'obliterate' Iranian power plants if Tehran does not fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with the deadline set for 23:44 GMT Monday

Iran's military operational command responded that any strikes on Iranian facilities would trigger attacks on 'all energy, information technology and desalination infrastructure belonging to the US' in the region
Iranian missiles struck two Israeli towns including Dimona, which houses a nuclear facility, wounding more than 100 people in one of the most destructive attacks since the war began February 28
Saudi Arabia and UAE reported fresh missile and drone attacks Monday morning, with Saudi Arabia detecting two ballistic missiles targeting Riyadh and an Indian national wounded in Abu Dhabi

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focus on humanitarian and economic impacts, detailed coverage of Gulf state attacks

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Iran's defiant response and continued military capability

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Highlighted IEA warnings and included live updates format

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of Gulf attacks and regional infrastructure threats

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Emphasized economic impacts on Asian markets

top_stories.headline: Global Energy Crisis Deepens as IEA Warns of Worst Shock in Decades
bullets: IEA chief Fatih Birol compared the current crisis to the 1970s oil shocks combined with the 2022 Ukraine impact, calling it 'two oil crises and one gas crash put all together'
Asian stock markets plunged Monday with Japan's Nikkei 225 falling 3.6% and South Korea's Kospi dropping nearly 6%, driven by heavy dependence on oil and gas transiting the Strait of Hormuz
Approximately 20% of global oil and liquefied natural gas normally passes through the strait, which Iran has effectively blocked since February 28
Le Monde analysis noted the crisis provides Russia 'unexpected economic and strategic breathing space' as oil prices surge past $100 per barrel

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Led with market impacts and IEA warnings

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Published analysis piece on Russian benefits from the crisis

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline focus on stock market falls

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Regional focus on Japan and South Korea economic impacts

top_stories.headline: Israel Approves Expansion of Ground and Air Operations Against Hezbollah in Lebanon
bullets: Israeli military chief Lt Gen Eyal Zamir approved plans for 'prolonged operation' to 'advance targeted ground operations and strikes' against Hezbollah

Israeli air strikes damaged the Qasmiye bridge over the Litani River, a key route connecting southern Lebanon to central areas, after Defense Minister Israel Katz ordered destruction of crossings used by Hezbollah
More than 1,000 people have been killed in Lebanon since Israel intensified its campaign, including at least 118 children and 40 health workers, with over one million displaced
The escalation follows Hezbollah rocket fire into Israel after the group's Iranian backers came under US-Israeli attack on February 28

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed military operational context and humanitarian toll

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Integrated into broader Iran war coverage

top_stories.headline: French Socialists Retain Major Cities as Municipal Elections Boost Mainstream Parties
bullets: Socialist Party and allies held Paris, Marseille, Lyon, and Lille, France's four largest cities, in second-round municipal elections Sunday
In Paris, Emmanuel Grégoire defeated right-wing former minister Rachida Dati with 51% to 40%, while far-left France Unbowed candidate Sonia Chikirou received approximately 8%

Socialist alliances with France Unbowed failed in traditional strongholds like Clermont-Ferrand and Brest, with PS secretary-general Pierre Jouvet stating 'LFI wins nothing – and what is worse it is the LFI that brings about defeat'
The far-right National Rally made gains in smaller cities, winning Nice through an ally of Marine Le Pen, but failed to capture major urban centers

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized lessons about far-left alliance failures

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Detailed breakdown of major city results

top_stories.headline: UN Climate Report Confirms Record Heat Trapping in 2025, Warns of Millennia-Long Consequences
bullets: The World Meteorological Organization reported Earth's energy imbalance reached record levels in 2025, with more heat trapped than at any point since observations began in 1960
The 11 hottest years on record all occurred between 2015 and 2025, with 2025 ranking as the second or third warmest year at 1.43°C above pre-industrial averages
Greenhouse gas concentrations of CO2, methane, and nitrous oxide have reached their highest levels in at least 800,000 years

UN Secretary-General António Guterres declared 'Planet Earth is being pushed beyond its limits. Every key climate indicator is flashing red' and called for transition from fossil fuels to renewable energy

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Included context on expected El Niño phase later this year

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the millennia-long duration of warming consequences

additional_stories.headline: CDU wins Rhineland-Palatinate after 35 years of SPD rule as AfD achieves record western Germany score of 20%
Tens of thousands rally in Prague against PM Babiš's government in largest Czech protest since 2019
Israeli settlers attack Palestinian villages in West Bank following death of 18-year-old settler, with over 20 attacks reported overnight
Army drone strike on El-Daein hospital in Sudan kills 64 and wounds 89, WHO reports
Air Canada Express jet collides with Port Authority vehicle at LaGuardia Airport, closing runway
UK bilateral aid to African countries to be cut by 56% (£900m) by 2028-29 to fund defense spending increase
Slovenia election too close to call as liberal PM Golob claims narrow victory over conservative challenger Janša
Madagascar's military president decrees new ministers must pass lie detector tests to root out corruption
North Korea's rubber-stamp legislature reelects Kim Jong Un as president of state affairs
Nigeria records 46% increase in terrorism deaths in 2025, rising to fourth place globally in terrorism index
